meaning: adj.深刻的,意义深远的,渊博的,造诣深的
examples: Her speech made a profound impact on everyone.
Colour has a profound, though often subliminal, influence on our senses and moods.
Recent studies have borne out claims that certain perfumes can bring about profound psychological changes.
Law and order matters in a way that is more profound than most commentary suggests.
Traversing the Bernese Oberland on a summer holiday had a profound effect on the 19-year-old author-to-be.
